role: Iranian diplomat expelled from Albania (2020); alleged MOIS operative
bio: Saeid Hassanzadeh was an Iranian diplomat stationed in Albania who was expelled in January 2020 in the second wave of Albanian expulsions of Iranian intelligence personnel — the first having been the expulsion of Gholamhossein Mohammadnia in 2018. Albanian authorities alleged that Hassanzadeh operated as a Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS) officer tasked with surveillance and targeting operations against members of the Mojahedin-e Khalq ([MEK](/orgs/mojahedin-e-khalq)) at their Ashraf-3 compound near Manez.
editorial_notes: Expulsion confirmed. MOIS operative status is the Albanian government's allegation. Do not link to Gholamhossein Mohammadnia — that entity is being created separately.
